notes: Send 5 starting villagers to sheep. Send Jeanne straight to gold: build the Mining Camp, then the first House, then mine gold.
Keep the Town Center making villagers nonstop while Jeanne starts the gold income for your Feudal landmark.
Put the next villagers onto wood for houses and the School of Cavalry follow-up.
Jeanne can stay on gold until you have enough for the age-up, then help build or return to food.
Most new villagers go to food so the Feudal click is not delayed.
Use Jeanne safely: do not lose her scouting or trying to fight too early.
With about 400 food and 200 gold, age up with School of Cavalry for early Royal Knights.
Use 3 to 4 villagers on the landmark; keep enough on food that villager production never stops.
During the age-up, balance onto food and gold so the first Royal Knight starts immediately.
Add wood only for houses and a second production building later.
Queue Royal Knights from School of Cavalry as soon as Feudal finishes.
Keep Jeanne near safe fights or scouts so she gains experience without dying.
Add more villagers to gold so Knights keep producing.
Your first raids should hit exposed gold, wood, or berries; leave before Spearmen trap you.
Use 3 to 4 Knights to force idle time, not to fight under the enemy Town Center.
If they overbuild Spearmen, add Archers or go Castle behind the pressure.
Build a second Stable only if food and gold are both stable.
Keep Jeanne active but protected; leveling her is a bonus, not worth throwing the game.
Research key cavalry upgrades and decide: keep Feudal pressure if they are damaged, or bank for Castle if they stabilize.
Your beginner rule: Knights harass, villagers scale, Jeanne stays alive.
